title: Mobile Responsiveness Fixes & Admin Login Button Label Update

requirements.summary: Make the entire public-facing site fully responsive on mobile devices
acceptanceCriteria: All public pages render correctly on 375px wide viewport (mobile)
No horizontal overflow or clipped content on mobile
Two-column About section layout stacks to single column on mobile
Services and Clinics grids collapse to 1 column on mobile
Header navigation is accessible and usable on mobile
Footer renders correctly without overflow on mobile
Touch targets (buttons, links) are at least 44px in height/width on mobile

file_operations.description: Audit and fix mobile responsiveness for the header component. Ensure navigation links, anchor links, and admin panel link are accessible on mobile. Fix touch target sizes to be at least 44px. Update responsive classes for mobile viewport (sm breakpoint). Ensure scroll-aware background blur works correctly on mobile and that the header doesn't cause horizontal overflow.

Fix mobile responsiveness for the hero section. Ensure the ParticleCanvas component scales correctly on mobile. Verify the hero image (frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-bg.dim_1920x1080.png or hero-bg.dim_1440x800.png) scales appropriately without overflow. Adjust site title typography and spacing for mobile viewports. Ensure the optional glow effect renders correctly on small screens.

Fix mobile layout for the About section. Convert the two-column layout to stack vertically on mobile (sm breakpoint and below). Ensure the profile image scales correctly and doesn't overflow. Fix text typography and spacing for mobile devices. Verify decorative accents render properly without causing layout issues on small screens.

Fix mobile responsiveness for the services grid. Ensure the grid collapses to a single column on mobile (sm breakpoint and below). Verify service cards render correctly with proper spacing and that icons display properly at mobile sizes. Ensure loading skeletons are also responsive and match the mobile grid layout.

Fix mobile responsiveness for the clinics grid. Ensure the grid collapses to a single column on mobile (sm breakpoint and below). Verify ClinicCard components render correctly on small screens with proper spacing. Ensure loading skeletons match the mobile grid layout.

Fix mobile layout and touch targets for clinic cards. Ensure all text content (name, description, address, phone) wraps correctly and doesn't overflow on mobile. Make action buttons (booking, map) have adequate touch target sizes (at least 44px). Verify card spacing and padding work on small screens.

Fix mobile responsiveness for the social media links section. Ensure links have adequate touch target sizes (at least 44px). Verify the layout and spacing work correctly on small screens. Ensure platform icons and text render properly without overflow on mobile.

Fix mobile responsiveness for the footer component. Ensure the particle canvas effect scales correctly on mobile. Verify optional glow orbs render properly on small screens. Ensure footer text content wraps correctly and doesn't cause horizontal overflow on mobile viewports. Fix any spacing or padding issues for mobile devices.

Fix canvas scaling and rendering on mobile devices. Ensure the particle system performs well on mobile and the canvas element doesn't cause horizontal overflow. Verify mouse interaction (if enabled) works with touch events on mobile. Adjust particle rendering parameters if needed for mobile performance.

Fix mobile responsiveness for glassmorphism card styling. Ensure padding, border radius, and hover effects work appropriately on mobile devices. Verify the card doesn't cause layout issues on small screens.

Add or update mobile-specific utility classes and media queries as needed to support responsive fixes across all components. Ensure glassmorphism card classes, gradient text, and other reusable styles work correctly at mobile breakpoints. Add any missing responsive utilities needed by the component fixes.

Review and adjust Tailwind breakpoint configuration if needed to ensure sm breakpoint and responsive utilities are properly configured for mobile responsiveness. Verify custom utilities (glow shadows, animations) work correctly at mobile sizes.

requirements.summary: Change the admin login button label from 'Admin' to 'Login'
acceptanceCriteria: The admin panel access button displays 'Login' as its label
Clicking the button still navigates to the admin panel / triggers the login flow as before
No changes to authentication logic, session storage, or login form behavior

file_operations.description: Update the admin panel access button label from 'Admin' to 'Login' in both desktop and mobile layouts. Keep all other button properties (styling, navigation behavior, click handlers) exactly the same. Do not modify any authentication, session, or routing logic.

If AdminPanel.tsx contains any button or link with the 'Admin' label that should be changed to 'Login', update it here. Otherwise, ensure no changes are needed. Keep all authentication state management, session token handling, and login/dashboard switching logic unchanged.
